articles.title: PM announces new powers to boost fuel supply amid Middle East tensions

children.content: The Albanese government will use public money to underwrite the delivery of extra cargoes of fuel in a bid to shore up supply during the Middle East conflict.

image.altText: An empty sign on petrol pumps

articles.title: For Australia, the end of the Iran war might just be the start

children.content: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

image.altText: A large ship sails along the coastline.
People with guns protesting.

children.content: Israel’s military says it has detected a missile launched from Yemen toward its territory and that its aerial defence systems were engaged to intercept the incoming threat after explosions were heard in Damascus and Beirut. Follow live.

items.title: Israel accuses Yemen of entering conflict, explosions in Beirut and Damascus

updates.title: Israel moves to intercept missile fired from Yemen
Israel says Yemen has fired its first missile since the war began
Israeli military says it's striking 'regime targets' in Tehran

items.title: 'Got it all wrong': The miscalculation the US and Israel made over Iran

children.content: Donald Trump is deploying thousands of marines and extra battleships to the Middle East, while also claiming to be close to a deal with Iran to end the war. So is he set to wind down his war with Iran, or is he thinking of escalating?

image.altText: Smoke rises over the skyline in Kuwait.

items.title: Trump under pressure to find an off-ramp amid oil and munitions crisis

children.content: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply, military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: Bank of America agrees to pay $105 million to settle Epstein-related lawsuit

children.content: The Bank of America has agreed to pay $US72.5 million ($105.3 million) to settle a civil lawsuit brought by women who accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, court records show.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with grey-white hair wearing a grey T-shirt looks serious in front of a plain background.

items.title: 'Like winning lotto': Medicare owes money to almost one million Aussies

children.content: Services Australia is urging people to check if they're owed money from a $272 million pool of unclaimed money.

image.altText: someone holds a medicare card

items.title: Cyclone Narelle devastates WA tourist town, airport 'obliterated'

children.content: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth is left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle rips through the town.

articles.title: WA gas facilities, ports suffer major disruptions after cyclone

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

articles.title: Thousands remain without power as ex-Cyclone Narelle continues south — as it happened

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting Western Australia.

image.altText: Tin roofs collapsed on carports with a boat and car in the foreground.

articles.title: 'Like a war zone': Exmouth picks up the pieces in the wake of Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

items.title: Dockers battle Tigers after Lions flex premiership muscle to beat Saints

children.content: Fremantle plays host to Richmond with one eye on the weather in Perth, while Brisbane has too much run and too much class for St Kilda in a 33-point win. Follow live.

image.altText: Shai Bolton handballs while being tackled by Sam Lalor

items.title: Marquee halfback clash poised to light up battle of the west

children.content: There's never any love lost when the Panthers play the Eels and a mouth-watering play-making duel between Nathan Cleary and Mitchell Moses beckons. Follow live.

items.title: Greek villages 'becoming ghost towns' as an alarming problem takes hold

children.content: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

items.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

items.title: Baby killed, woman and child injured as rideshare car hits tree

children.content: A 7-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

image.altText: A close up view of police tape, with emergency service vehicle lights in the background.

items.title: Critical bridges across Lebanon's Litani River targeted in calculated invasion plan

children.content: Israel's expanded invasion of southern Lebanon was at least 12 days in the making, with bridges targeted to effectively cut off the south from the country's centre.

image.altText: thumbnail IDF Dlafy

articles.title: US 'on verge' of ending Iran war as Rubio demands allies lead postwar coalition

children.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells G7 leaders to ready a postwar coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the US-Israeli war on Iran could conclude within weeks.

image.altText: Marco Rubio addresses reporters on the tarmac infront of a plane.

items.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home

children.content: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: Night events for female runners in NSW after harrassment reported

children.content: Fifty per cent of female runners in NSW
have experienced harassment, new research shows, with an overwhelming majority saying safety issues dictate their behaviour.

image.altText: a group women at a race track stand at the starting line

items.title: As music festivals collapse, one scrappy underdog is making a lot of noise

children.content: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

items.title: The nine books that knocked our critics' socks off this month

items.title: Newlyweds sell everything to buy 160-year-old former pub

children.content: A 160-year-old hotel sat on the market for four years, until Jason and Kyla Romaior decided to take a risk. Insurance costs are $20,000 a year, but they are confident they have what it takes to handle the challenges of owning a heritage building.

image.altText: couple leaning over fence on verandah of wooden building, smiling

items.title: Lambs hit $500 as supply dwindles towards record low

children.content: Australian sheep and lamb prices keep rising despite global uncertainty. But how long will consumers be willing to pay?

image.altText: A pen full of lambs.

items.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

image.altText: A middle-aged man with a white bandage on his head. Telegram messages and images fade in behind him.

items.title: Tristan Gourlay survives onboard fire to break Australian record for fastest global circumnavigation

children.content: Tristan Gourlay has broken the Australian sailing record for the fastest solo, unassisted circumnavigation of the world — completing it in 138 days. The record he broke was set by his father, Ken Gourlay, in 2007.

thumbnail.altText: A man raises his arms in triumph on a boat

avatar.alt: A woman with dark hair smiles.

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

image.altText: Two tubes of sunscreen with 50+ SPF showing.

items.title: Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah speaks at NSW writers' festival

children.content: Her inclusion at the event was slammed by NSW Premier Chris Minns, but Dr Abdel-Fattah told an audience in Newcastle today that art and literature can be spaces for political expression.

image.altText: an author, holding a microphone, speaking to an audience

items.title: Three killed in a spate of road crashes across north and central Queensland

children.content: Queensland has recorded three new traffic fatalities over the past 24 hours. There were 64 fatalities recorded in the state from January 1 to March 22, compared to 49 in the same period last year. 2025 marked a 16-year high for road deaths.

image.altText: Police tape in the foreground with trees and car in the background

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

items.title: 'Gigantic waves' batter NSW coastline amid 'treacherous' surf conditions

children.content: Huge swells have inundated NSW beaches as most of the coastline remains closed to the public due to hazardous surf conditions.

items.title: Isolated school students make sacrifices amid fuel crisis

children.content: Ruben Driver has a round trip of 4,000 kilometres to reach his boarding school. The cost of fuel is placing his family under immense pressure to get him to class.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

items.title: Ranked number one on the terrorism index, this nation struck back

children.content: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.
